Shenzhen Woer Heat-Shrinkable Material Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:9981) ) has shared an update.

Shenzhen Woer Heat-Shrinkable Material Co., Ltd. has released its unaudited first quarterly report for 2026, prepared under China Accounting Standards for Business Enterprises as of March 31, 2026. The company states that its board, senior management and accounting leadership collectively guarantee the truthfulness, accuracy and completeness of the disclosed financial information.

The announcement, issued in both Chinese and English with the Chinese version prevailing in case of discrepancy, complies with Hong Kong listing and securities regulations. The filing reinforces the group’s regulatory transparency and governance practices, signaling ongoing adherence to disclosure obligations for investors across its dual listings in Shenzhen and Hong Kong.

More about Shenzhen Woer Heat-Shrinkable Material Co., Ltd. Class H

Shenzhen Woer Heat-Shrinkable Material Co., Ltd. is a PRC-incorporated joint stock company listed in both Shenzhen and Hong Kong, operating under stock codes 002130 and 9981. The group focuses on heat-shrinkable materials and related products, serving industrial and electrical applications in domestic and international markets.

data.title: China Oriental Sets AGM to Approve Dividends, Board Mandates and Share Issue Authority
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from China Oriental Group Co ( (HK:0581) ) is now available.

China Oriental Group Co has convened its annual general meeting for 10 June 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will be asked to adopt the audited consolidated financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025 and approve a final dividend of HK$0.02 per share and a special dividend of HK$0.05 per share. Investors will also vote on the re-election of four directors, including two long-serving independent non-executive directors, the re-appointment of Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu as auditor, and a general mandate allowing the board to issue up to 20% of the company’s share capital, which would give management flexibility for future capital-raising or strategic transactions while potentially diluting existing holdings.

The proposed share issuance mandate, excluding treasury shares and certain standard exceptions such as rights issues and share schemes, is designed to remain in force until the next annual general meeting. These resolutions, if passed, will shape the company’s governance structure, capital management options and shareholder returns policy for the coming year, underscoring a balance between rewarding investors via dividends and preserving room for corporate and financing activities in the Hong Kong market.

More about China Oriental Group Co

China Oriental Group Co is a Bermuda-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong under stock code 581. The group operates in the industrial sector and is overseen by a board comprising executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, with its financial reporting audited by Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu.

The latest announcement is out from Shenzhen Woer Heat-Shrinkable Material Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:9981) ).

Shenzhen Woer Heat-Shrinkable Material Co., Ltd. has announced a final ordinary cash dividend of RMB 1.65 per 10 shares for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, subject to shareholder approval on 26 May 2026. The dividend for Hong Kong-listed shares will be paid in Hong Kong dollars on 31 July 2026, with the exact HKD amount, exchange rate, ex-dividend date, record date, and related transfer deadlines to be confirmed.

The company has detailed withholding tax arrangements for different categories of shareholders, including PRC and non-PRC enterprises, overseas individual investors, and northbound and southbound trading investors, with rates generally ranging from 10% to 20%. This payout underlines the firm’s profit distribution policy and provides visibility on shareholder returns, while tax treatment differences may influence net income for various investor groups and could guide portfolio positioning for both mainland and Hong Kong market participants.

More about Shenzhen Woer Heat-Shrinkable Material Co., Ltd. Class H

Shenzhen Woer Heat-Shrinkable Material Co., Ltd. operates in the materials and components sector, focusing on heat-shrinkable products used widely in electrical insulation, wiring protection, and related industrial applications. The company targets both domestic Chinese and international markets, positioning itself as a specialized supplier to infrastructure, electronics, and power-related industries.

An announcement from Shandong Extreme Vision Technology Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:6636) ) is now available.

Shandong Extreme Vision Technology has adopted an electronic-first model for distributing corporate communications to shareholders, following updated Hong Kong listing rules that took effect at the end of 2023. Future reports, circulars and meeting notices will be published on the company’s website and the Hong Kong stock exchange disclosure platform, replacing printed copies unless investors specifically opt in.

Non-registered shareholders who prefer hard copies can submit a request form or write to the H share registrar to receive printed English, Chinese or bilingual versions for a limited period. The move is intended to shift investors toward self-service online access, streamline distribution, and may reduce the company’s printing and mailing costs while modernising its investor relations practices.

data.title: RUSAL Pays RUB 426.3 Million Coupon on BO-001P-09 Exchange-Traded Bonds
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

United Co RUSAL ( (HK:0486) ) has issued an update.

United Company RUSAL has disclosed that it paid coupon income on its BO-001P-09 series exchange-traded bonds for the twenty-second coupon period running from 24 March 2026 to 23 April 2026. The total payout amounted to RUB 426.3 million, with bondholders receiving RUB 14.21 per bond across 30 million bonds.

The coupon on these exchange-traded bonds is calculated daily over the circulation period, using the Bank of Russia key rate as of the seventh day before the calculation date plus an additional 2.20% per annum S-spread. The announcement underscores RUSAL’s continued servicing of its ruble-denominated debt instruments on the Moscow Exchange, signalling ongoing access to and activity in the domestic bond market.

More about United Co RUSAL

United Company RUSAL is an international public joint-stock company and a major producer in the global aluminium industry. Listed in Hong Kong and on Russian exchanges, the group focuses on producing aluminium and related products for industrial and manufacturing customers worldwide.

data.title: New Gonow RV Posts Flat 2025 Revenue but Margin Squeeze as Hybrid Push and Market Downturn Bite
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An announcement from New Gonow Recreational Vehicles, Inc. ( (HK:0805) ) is now available.

New Gonow Recreational Vehicles reported audited annual results for 2025 showing revenue of RMB871.4 million, largely flat year-on-year, while gross profit fell 11.7% to RMB237.2 million and gross margin narrowed from 31.1% to 27.2%. Management attributed the margin pressure to macroeconomic volatility, an unexpected downturn in the RV market, and the lower initial margins from its new hybrid model, even as cost of sales rose 6.5% on higher volumes.

The company indicated that trading in its shares will resume following the publication of these results, marking a step toward normalizing its status on the Hong Kong market. The combination of resilient top-line performance but weaker profitability underscores both the challenges in the current RV cycle and the near-term cost of pursuing market expansion with new product lines and dealership arrangements in key regions such as Australasia.

More about New Gonow Recreational Vehicles, Inc.

New Gonow Recreational Vehicles Inc., incorporated in the Cayman Islands and listed in Hong Kong, operates in the recreational vehicle industry, focusing on RV products including a new hybrid model and electric RVs for markets such as China and Australasia. The group distributes through joint-venture dealers and stores, and leverages partnerships like its Green RV dealership agreement in Australia to expand its international footprint.

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

USAS Building System (Shanghai) Co.,Ltd. ( (HK:2671) ) has issued an update.

USAS Building System (Shanghai) Co., Ltd. plans to abolish its supervisory committee and transfer its oversight functions to the audit committee of the board, aligning its governance structure with the newly revised PRC Company Law and prevailing regulatory practices. The company has stated that the outgoing supervisors have no disagreements with the board, and all references to the supervisory committee in internal rules will be removed once the changes take effect.

To support this shift, the company is proposing amendments to its Articles of Association, including deleting supervisory committee provisions, updating shareholder approval matters to align with Hong Kong listing rules, and making other housekeeping changes. These amendments will be put to shareholders for approval as a special resolution at the May 15, 2026 annual general meeting, with subsequent regulatory filings to be handled by the board once the final version is cleared by authorities.

More about USAS Building System (Shanghai) Co.,Ltd.

USAS Building System (Shanghai) Co., Ltd. is a Mainland China-based joint stock company listed in Hong Kong, operating in the construction and building systems sector. Shanghai Longcheer Technology Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:9611) ) has issued an update.

Shanghai Longcheer Technology Co., Ltd. has provided further details on its discloseable transaction to acquire a 60% equity interest in certain target companies, following an earlier announcement in March 2026. Zhejiang Expressway Co has signed a cooperation agreement with Technology Research Institute, ZJIC, Zhejiang Information and Commercial Group to form a new joint venture focused on low-altitude infrastructure. Seacon Shipping Group Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:2409) ) has provided an update.

Seacon Shipping Group Holdings Ltd. has agreed to sell two 48,785-gross-ton bulk carriers, Seacon Vancouver and Seacon Oslo, both built in 2023, to an independent buyer for a total consideration of USD72.7 million. The vessels are currently chartered to Seacon’s subsidiaries under bareboat charters, and the company intends to first acquire them by exercising existing purchase options before delivering them to the buyer.

The disposal and the exercise of each purchase option each qualify as discloseable transactions under Hong Kong Listing Rules, with the relevant percentage ratios exceeding 5% but below 25%. The deals underscore Seacon’s active management of its fleet portfolio and capital structure, and they trigger reporting and announcement obligations without requiring shareholder approval, signaling a moderate but notable rebalancing of its vessel assets for investors to monitor.

More about Seacon Shipping Group Holdings Ltd.

Seacon Shipping Group Holdings Ltd. is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong that operates in the maritime shipping industry, focusing on owning and chartering bulk carrier vessels. Its fleet includes modern bulk carriers that are typically deployed under bareboat and other chartering arrangements to serve global dry bulk cargo trade.

Asiaray Media Group Limited announced changes to its board, with non-executive director Wu Xiaopin resigning effective 23 April 2026 to concentrate on other business commitments. The company emphasized that Wu has no disagreement with the board and that there are no issues arising from her departure requiring shareholder attention.
